verdicts.explanation: The two surviving diff lines are nothing but the single-file bundle wrapper the wt_ prompt generator emits around the module-under-test: `<file path="lib/file_upload.ex">` … `</file>`. Everything between them is byte-for-byte identical to the current parent solution.ex — I diffed prompt.md lines 59-257 against solution.ex lines 1-199 and they are IDENTICAL, which is also why the checker surfaced ONLY the two marker lines. The parent solution.ex is NOT a bundle (grep for `<file path=` returns 0), so there is no matching parent marker for the diff to cancel against. This is a mechanical, precisely nameable pattern the checker already recognizes: the wrapper lines match its own `@bundle_marker` regex `^</?file( path=.*)?>$` (check_embeds.exs:96), and it already strips exactly these lines from the PARENT side for module-FIM bundles via `drop_markers` (check_embeds.exs:284,286,352-360). The design comment at check_embeds.exs:46 says 'wt_ embeds keep the markers — no allowance', but that assumption only holds when the parent is itself a bundle (parent markers then match the embed markers → CLEAN, as seen for wt_016/018/019/021/102). For a NON-bundle parent the wt_ generator still wraps the module in a single-file `<file>` tag, so the wrapper has no counterpart in the parent and registers as drift. The wrapper is a systematic generator convention, not staleness: it appears identically across every non-bundle-parent wt_ dir (wt_020, wt_022, wt_023, wt_024, wt_025), and the wt_ prompt.md (mtime 2026-07-10 16:26) is NEWER than the de-bundled parent solution.ex (mtime 16:20), so the wrapper was added by the generator after the parent was already in its current marker-free form — it is not leftover residue from a de-bundling of the parent. No code, docstring, or comment text differs, so this is not real_drift; the checker simply fails to strip a wrapper pattern it strips everywhere else.
